Title: AKR2A participates in the regulation of cotton fibre development by modulating biosynthesis of very-long-chain fatty acids

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAE1/KCS condensing enzyme family; transmembrane domain-containing VLCFA elongase component
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Fatty acid elongase complex; VLCFA elongation module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA with long-chain acyl-CoA substrates to initiate each two-carbon elongation cycle in VLCFA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms VLCFA elongation intermediates required for target-product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for cotton fibre elongation and development; associated with membrane expansion and signalling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes VLCFA accumulation and increases VLCFA/short-chain fatty acid ratio; co-overexpression with AKR2A gives the highest VLCFA and oil levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KCS1 was identified as a key VLCFA-biosynthetic enzyme interacting with AKR2A in vitro and in vivo. 2. KCS1 overexpression increased seed oil and VLCFA levels in Arabidopsis. 3. AKR2A/KCS1 co-overexpression caused the strongest increase in VLCFAs, supporting synergistic control of VLCFA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/committed-step enzyme in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis; transcript and protein elevated in KCS1-overexpressing and AKR2A/KCS1 co-overexpressing lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: KCS-family transcripts were highly upregulated during fast cotton fibre elongation, especially around 15 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fibre developmental stage (especially 15 DPA); transgenic overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; co-overexpression with AKR2A; qRT-PCR/RNA blot; Western blot; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme target for engineering higher VLCFA production and improved fibre traits, especially in combination with AKR2A
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in cotton fibre (study-level, accession not reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In cotton, KCS1 is mainly implicated through upregulated KCS-family transcripts and pathway model; direct Arabidopsis KCS1 evidence is strongest in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: VLCFA biosynthesis and downstream hormone/redox-associated metabolic signalling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of VLCFA biosynthesis; interaction-mediated modulation of KCS1; transcriptional upregulation of VLCFA-, ethylene-, auxin-, H2O2-, cell wall- and cytoskeleton-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: KCS1; KCS2; KCS6; KCS12; KCS13; KCR1; KCR2; KCR3; ECR1; ECR2; ACO1; ACO2; ACS; GhPIN3a; GhPIN3b; APX1; POD; EXP1; TUB1; ACT1; CER6; LCB1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/upregulates transcript accumulation of VLCFA biosynthetic genes and multiple downstream ethylene, auxin transport, ROS homeostasis, and fibre-elongation genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with KCS1; proposed molecular scaffold for fatty acid elongase complex
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote cotton fibre elongation/development; support membrane expansion and signalling that affects ethylene, auxin and H2O2 pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical positive regulator that elevates VLCFA content and VLCFA/short-chain fatty acid ratio, thereby promoting fibre elongation and associated fibre quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases fibre initiation, elongation, fibre length, uniformity and strength, and elevates VLCFA and oil accumulation; mutants show reduced oil/VLCFA levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis AKR2A overexpression in cotton significantly promoted fibre initiation and elongation and improved mature fibre quality. 2. AKR2A increased VLCFA content and VLCFA/short-chain fatty acid ratio in cotton fibres and Arabidopsis seeds. 3. AKR2A interacted with KCS1 in vitro and in vivo, and AKR2A/KCS1 co-overexpression produced the highest VLCFA levels. 4. AKR2A upregulated genes linked to ethylene biosynthesis, auxin transport/signalling, H2O2 homeostasis, cell wall and cytoskeleton, consistent with a signalling cascade downstream of VLCFAs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in cotton and Arabidopsis; Arabidopsis tilling mutants; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Western blot; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; metabolite/enzyme assays; ion-selective flux measurement
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fibre developmental stages 0-25 DPA, especially 10-15 DPA; field and in vitro ovule culture conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic cotton, AKR2A transcript was strongly elevated, especially at 10-15 DPA fibres (>50-fold vs WT)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate gene for cotton fibre yield/quality improvement via metabolic engineering to boost VLCFA-mediated fibre elongation, potentially stacked with KCS1
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious whole-plant phenotype in transgenic cotton; Arabidopsis akr2a mutants showed chlorotic seedling leaves associated with reduced fatty acid levels
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 15 DPA cotton fibres (accession not reported)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although source gene is from Arabidopsis, the applied metabolic phenotype was mainly evaluated in transgenic cotton; authors propose AKR2A acts upstream of ethylene, auxin and H2O2 through VLCFA modulation.
